title: antiphylla retusa
intro: Antiphylla retusa, also known as the 'broken leaf', is a plant species that belongs to the family of Rubiaceae and is commonly found in tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Antiphylla retusa include reducing pain and swelling, improving digestion, and relieving respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Antiphylla retusa typically involve infusing the leaves in hot water to make a tea or using the plant in decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
